sourceMovie.title: The Lego Batman Movie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Lego Batman Movie (2017) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lego Batman Movie clicked for you, The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ice Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lego Batman Movie clicked for you, Ice Age (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lego Batman Movie?
faq.answer: Try The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part, Ice Age, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit—each shares DNA with The Lego Batman Movie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lego Batman Movie?
faq.answer: The Lego Batman Movie (2017) is best described as Animation, Action, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lego Batman Movie worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
